raw_text:
```json
{
  "claim_texts": [
    "Universal and early access to computer science education is critical to providing California’s students with the skills they need to succeed in the 21st century economy.",
    "The digital divide in computer science education is often felt the most in low-income communities and school districts.",
    "The state of California has adopted its first-ever computer science standards.",
    "California is considering adopting a Computer Science Strategic Implementation Plan (CSSIP) to broaden the pool of computer science teachers and ensure quality education for K-12 students.",
    "Assembly Bill 20 would create the California Computer Science Coordinator role within the California Department of Education.",
    "Assembly Bill 52 would require the CSSIP to be continually updated to reflect technological advancements.",
    "The U.S. faces a significant and growing computer science skills gap.",
    "There are nearly 571,000 open computing jobs nationwide, while only slightly more than 49,000 students graduated with computer science degrees last year.",
    "Introducing computer science courses at a young age has the potential to close the diversity gap in computer science fields.",
    "Statistics indicate that young women who take an Advanced Placement computer science course in high school are 10 times as likely to major in computer science in college.",
    "African American and Latino students are over seven times more likely to major in computer science if they take an Advanced Placement computer science course in high school."
  ]
}
```
